DATA.title: Gandhi's Political Thought: Philosophy for Praxis
AI for Impact: Data Monitoring & Evaluation
MEL for Development: Monitoring, Evaluation & Learning
Politics of Aspiration: Rights, Insurance & Social Mobility
Media for Development: Communication, Power & Practice
Item Response Theory and Qualitative Assessment
The Handbook of Social Protection (Hanna & Olken)

English for Development.desc: Bridges language proficiency with development discourse through situated learning—moving beyond grammar drills to comprehend development literature, policy documents, and cross-cultural communication in aid organizations.
English for Development.quote: Finally understood development policy documents without constantly checking dictionaries. The situated approach made academic prose accessible.

Observation to Insight.desc: Equips researchers with structured frameworks for converting qualitative observations into actionable analytical insights without falling into confirmation bias or anecdotal reasoning.
Observation to Insight.quote: My field notes went from anecdotal to analytical. The framework prevented me from over-interpreting patterns that weren't really there.

Economics 101: A Policy Primer.desc: Demystifies core economic principles specifically contextualized for development policymakers—elasticity, general equilibrium, and behavioral economics in non-OECD settings.
Economics 101: A Policy Primer.quote: As a non-economist, I could finally follow policy briefings on subsidy design and market interventions. Highly practical.

Data Feminism.desc: Interrogates how data collection, interpretation, and visualization practices reflect and reinforce gender hierarchies; examines algorithmic bias in development monitoring systems.
Data Feminism.quote: Completely shifted how I think about my gender indicators. Never noticed the subtle bias in how we were measuring 'empowerment.'

Education and Pedagogy.desc: Synthesizes learning sciences with development education—cognition, constructivism, and pedagogical innovation for resource-constrained contexts.
Education and Pedagogy.quote: As an education program manager, this gave me language to explain *why* our participatory methods work, not just that they do.

Climate Essentials.desc: Translates climate science for development practitioners: climate systems, mitigation-adaptation tradeoffs, and finance mechanisms central to NDCs.
Climate Essentials.quote: I can now speak credibly about NDCs and climate finance with my government counterparts. Very practical without being reductive.

Public Health 101.desc: Covers disease epidemiology, health systems strengthening, and public health surveillance with emphasis on fragile-state contexts and informal sector health.
Public Health 101.quote: Working in a fragile state, this was the first course that actually acknowledged our context rather than generic public health.

Indian Constitution and Development.desc: Analyzes constitutional provisions shaping development outcomes—fundamental rights, directive principles, federalism—and their implementation challenges.
Indian Constitution and Development.quote: My advocacy work is now grounded in constitutional argument, not just moral persuasion. Much stronger case-making.

Development Economics.desc: Advanced treatment of poverty traps, human capital accumulation, institutional constraints, and structural transformation using canonical and contemporary frameworks.
Development Economics.quote: PhD-level rigor but actually digestible for practitioners. The institutional constraints module changed how I design interventions.

Political Economy.desc: Examines power structures, elite capture, and institutional arrangements affecting development trajectories—beyond technocratic solutions.
Political Economy.quote: Stopped me from designing policies in an institutional vacuum. Now I seriously map power and incentive structures first.

Gender Studies 101.desc: Unpacks gender as intersectional identity and social system; covers feminist economics, care work, and gender-transformative development approaches.
Gender Studies 101.quote: Introduced me to feminist economics concepts I'd been grasping for. The intersectionality framework sharpened our program design.

Research Ethics.desc: Navigate institutional ethics review, informed consent in asymmetric settings, data rights, and researcher positionality in global development research.
Research Ethics.quote: Navigated a complex ethics review for our fragile-state study with confidence. Reduced post-hoc complications significantly.

Women's Economic Empowerment.desc: Synthesizes evidence on WEE pathways: productive assets, financial inclusion, market access, and the measurement of empowerment beyond income metrics.
Women's Economic Empowerment.quote: Moved our WEE program beyond income metrics to actual agency. The measurement framework prevented us from declaring false empowerment.

Livelihoods Fundamentals.desc: Grounded in sustainable livelihoods framework—analyzing assets, vulnerabilities, and livelihood strategies in agrarian and urban informal economies.
Livelihoods Fundamentals.quote: Finally have a coherent way to talk about informal economy dynamics with both rural and urban program teams.

Advocacy Fundamentals.desc: Covers theory of change in advocacy, campaign design, stakeholder mapping, and evidence-based influencing for policy and social change.
Advocacy Fundamentals.quote: Our advocacy campaigns now have explicit ToCs. Makes tracking attribution and learning much more rigorous.

MEL Fundamentals.desc: Integrated treatment of monitoring (accountability), learning (adaptation), and evaluation (evidence); for adaptive development programs.
MEL Fundamentals.quote: Unified our fragmented M&E, monitoring, and learning systems. Team speaks the same language now.

Visual Ethnography.desc: Methodological foundations for using photography and video in ethnographic research; ethical considerations in representing communities visually.
Visual Ethnography.quote: Added video ethnography to our toolkit with actual ethical frameworks, not just intuition. Community benefited from better consent processes.

Poverty and Inequality.desc: Unpacks multidimensional poverty measures, inequality decomposition, and capability deprivation—moving beyond income-based definitions.
Poverty and Inequality.quote: Our multidimensional poverty assessments now reflect actual capability deficits, not just income poverty. More targeted interventions.

Data Literacy for Development.desc: Enables practitioners to read, critique, and appropriately deploy quantitative evidence without technical modeling; includes statistical literacy and data visualization.
Data Literacy for Development.quote: As a non-quantitative person, I can now challenge weak data claims in board meetings with credible arguments.

Sexual Rights and Health Basics.desc: Grounded in rights-based approaches to reproductive health, bodily autonomy, and gender-affirming care; addresses SDG 3.7 and health equity.
Sexual Rights and Health Basics.quote: Shifted our SRHR program from service delivery to rights framing. Deeper community trust and better outcomes.

Marginalized Identities and Development.desc: Examines caste, religion, ethnicity, disability, and other dimensions of social marginalization in development outcomes and program design.
Marginalized Identities and Development.quote: Our inclusion strategies were tokenistic until this. Now we actually address structural marginalization, not just representation.

Decolonizing Development.desc: Critical examination of development discourse genealogy; interrogates universalism, Western expertise, and alternative knowledge systems.
Decolonizing Development.quote: Forced difficult conversations in our team about whose knowledge counts. Program design is now genuinely bicultural.

Community-Led Development.desc: Shifts agency to communities—participatory design, community mobilization, and critiques of extractive development practice.
Community-Led Development.quote: Recognized extractive patterns in our own practice. Community engagement went from consultation theater to genuine co-design.

Environmental Justice.desc: Connects environmental sustainability with equity; addresses environmental racism, resource extraction, and climate justice in development context.
Environmental Justice.quote: Our climate adaptation program now explicitly addresses who bears the burden. Prevents us from climate-washing.

Care Economy and Unpaid Work.desc: Quantifies and values unpaid care work; examines care as infrastructure for human development and implications for gender equity.
Care Economy and Unpaid Work.quote: Put numbers on what we intuitively knew about care work burdens. Now governments take our advocacy on care policy seriously.

Econometrics 101.desc: Rigorous foundation in regression analysis, causal inference, and econometric identification strategies for development research.
Econometrics 101.quote: Moved from correlations to causal claims confidently. My impact assessments are now methodologically defensible.

Global Development Architecture.desc: Maps global development governance, multilateral institutions, development finance flows, and coordination mechanisms.
Global Development Architecture.quote: Finally understand who does what in the global development architecture. Navigate multilateral funding landscapes more strategically.

Post-Truth Politics.desc: Analyzes misinformation ecosystems, polarization, and their implications for evidence-based development policy and public discourse.
Post-Truth Politics.quote: Our evidence communications now account for information disorder. Fewer false certainties, better messaging.

Fundraising Fundamentals.desc: From theory of change to donor engagement—grant writing, proposal design, and relationship management for mission-driven organizations.
Fundraising Fundamentals.quote: Systematized our fundraising beyond relationship luck. Multi-year funding improved after applying the frameworks.

Qualitative Research Methods.desc: Depth qualitative approaches: interview design, thematic coding, phenomenology, and grounded theory with rigor and reflexivity.
Qualitative Research Methods.quote: My qualitative work gained academic rigor without losing nuance. Reviewers stopped challenging my methodology.

EDA for Humanitarian/Health/Social Data.desc: Exploratory data analysis techniques calibrated for messy real-world data; data cleaning and pattern recognition in health and social datasets.
EDA for Humanitarian/Health/Social Data.quote: Real-world health data is messy. This course taught me data cleaning without pretending data is cleaner than it is.

Bivariate Analysis.desc: Statistical foundations for assessing relationships between two variables; hypothesis testing and effect sizes in development contexts.
Bivariate Analysis.quote: Staff without stats background now understand p-values and effect sizes. Quality of data interpretation improved.

BCC and Communications.desc: Behavior Change Communication and strategic messaging in public health and development campaigns; audience research and creative messaging.
BCC and Communications.quote: Our health campaigns went from preachy to persuasive. Actually talk to audiences first, now.

Decent Work for All.desc: Covers ILO frameworks for quality employment: living wages, labor rights, social protection, and formalizing informal sector work.
Decent Work for All.quote: Our labor programs now reference ILO standards. Legitimizes our work in policy conversations.

Item Response Theory and Qualitative Ass.desc: Advanced measurement theory for constructing and validating assessment instruments; rasch models and test calibration.
Item Response Theory and Qualitative Ass.quote: Built valid learning assessments for our education program. Measurement error now explicitly managed.

Digital Development Ethics.desc: Examines algorithmic governance, digital divide, data privacy, and ethical technology deployment in development contexts.
Digital Development Ethics.quote: Digital transformation projects now actually incorporate ethics frameworks, not bolt them on at the end.

Multivariate Analysis.desc: Extensions beyond bivariate statistics: factor analysis, cluster analysis, and multivariate hypothesis testing for complex development phenomena.
Multivariate Analysis.quote: Could finally distinguish between correlation, confounding, and causation in multi-factor evaluations.

Social Emotional Learning.desc: Links SEL to capability development; covers self-awareness, relationship skills, and emotional regulation in educational and community settings.
Social Emotional Learning.quote: SEL went from soft-skill rhetoric to capability theory. Parents and teachers now understand the actual development impact.

Public Good Game.desc: Interactive simulation demonstrating free-rider problems and collective action dilemmas; explores how incentive structures affect cooperation.
Public Good Game.quote: Showed our board the tragedy of cooperation in 20 minutes. Much better than talking about free-riders abstractly.

Prisoners' Dilemma Game.desc: Playable exploration of strategic interdependence and the tension between individual and collective rationality in conflict situations.
Prisoners' Dilemma Game.quote: Communities actually experience the paradox rather than just hearing about it. Behavior change conversations deepened.

Opportunity Cost Game.desc: Embodied learning of resource scarcity and trade-off decisions; illustrates how constrained choices operate in development planning.
Opportunity Cost Game.quote: Finally, stakeholders viscerally understand budget constraints aren't arbitrary. Planning conversations more realistic.

Network Effects Game.desc: Demonstrates how value creation depends on network scale; relevant for understanding technology adoption and value chains.
Network Effects Game.quote: Tech adoption behavior suddenly made sense to non-tech staff. Better market-system analysis after this.

Cooperation Paradox Game.desc: Explores the tension between rational self-interest and cooperative outcomes; investigates when cooperation emerges spontaneously.
Cooperation Paradox Game.quote: Participants experienced when cooperation is rational vs. when it requires trust-building. Shifted our trust-building strategies.

Risk and Reward Game.desc: Experiential learning in risk assessment and portfolio management; relevant for understanding livelihood diversification and microfinance.
Risk and Reward Game.quote: Microfinance clients finally got why they shouldn't put all resources in one enterprise. Diversification uptake improved.

Bidding Wars Game.desc: Auction simulation revealing winner's curse and strategic bidding; applicable to procurement and resource allocation contexts.
Bidding Wars Game.quote: Procurement team experienced winner's curse firsthand. Bidding strategies dramatically improved after this.

Information Asymmetry Game.desc: Interactive demonstration of adverse selection and moral hazard in markets with incomplete information.
Information Asymmetry Game.quote: Market systems teams now truly grasp information failures. Supply-side interventions became much more targeted.

Economics Concepts Game.desc: Gamified puzzles integrating supply-demand, elasticity, and welfare economics in applied scenarios.
Economics Concepts Game.quote: Non-economists loved this. It bridged conceptual understanding to policy simulation.

Externality Game.desc: Illustrates positive and negative externalities and market failures through interactive resource management.
Externality Game.quote: Staff understood why our market intervention was needed after they experienced negative externalities in-game.

Commons Crisis Game.desc: Tragedy of the commons simulation demonstrating collective resource management dilemmas and governance mechanisms.
Commons Crisis Game.quote: Communities experienced the commons dynamic and designed governance solutions themselves. Much more ownership.

Code Convert Pro.desc: Statistical code translation and optimization tool converting between R, Python, Stata, and SQL; aids researchers switching platforms.
Code Convert Pro.quote: Migrated to R without rewriting all my analysis code. Saved weeks of work.

Qual Insights Lab.desc: Interactive workbench for thematic analysis; supports coding, memo-writing, and pattern identification from qualitative data.
Qual Insights Lab.quote: Analysis became transparent and auditable. Team understood my coding logic and could iterate on it.

Real Middle India.desc: Exploratory data visualization platform for Indian socioeconomic data; enables custom analysis of income, education, and livelihood trends.
Real Middle India.quote: Stopped guessing about middle-income dynamics. Data-driven arguments about targeting and policy now defensible.

Risk Mitigation Lab.desc: Scenario planning tool for identifying, assessing, and developing mitigation strategies for program risks.
Risk Mitigation Lab.quote: Systematic risk mapping replaced ad-hoc crisis response. Funder confidence in our risk management improved.

Policy Advocacy Lab.desc: Strategic planning workbench for advocacy campaigns; includes stakeholder mapping, timeline planning, and messaging frameworks.
Policy Advocacy Lab.quote: Evidence-based advocacy campaigns with explicit logic. Success rate and attribution tracking measurably improved.

Resource Sustainability Lab.desc: Analyzes resource flows, sustainability of financing models, and long-term organizational viability.
Resource Sustainability Lab.quote: First time we honestly mapped our resource dependency and sustainability vulnerabilities. Prompted strategic shifts.

Impact Partnerships.desc: Partnership mapping and collaboration design tool for multi-stakeholder initiatives; assesses complementarities and alignment.
Impact Partnerships.quote: Partnership assessment beyond feel-good intentions. Identified misalignments early, saved conflicts later.

MEL Design Lab.desc: Framework builder for constructing theory of change, results chains, and evaluation questions.
MEL Design Lab.quote: Our results chain went from vague to testable. Evaluation now actually measures what we claim to do.

MEL Plan Lab.desc: Operational planning tool for monitoring systems: indicator selection, data collection design, and analysis timelines.
MEL Plan Lab.quote: Built monitoring from first principles. Data quality improved because collection was purposefully designed.

Design Thinking Lab.desc: Structured facilitation tool for human-centered problem-solving; guides ideation and rapid prototyping.
Design Thinking Lab.quote: Moved from assumption-driven design to evidence-based iteration. Prototypes better matched actual user needs.

Theory of Change Workbench.desc: Interactive diagramming and narrative tool for constructing, testing, and refining theories of change.
Theory of Change Workbench.quote: Made our implicit assumptions explicit and testable. Board and team aligned on causal narrative.

Storytelling Lab.desc: Guides crafting of evidence-based impact narratives; balances anecdote with data for donor and stakeholder communication.
Storytelling Lab.quote: Impact stories now grounded in data without losing emotional resonance. Donor engagement significantly increased.

Community Engagement Lab.desc: Designs participatory engagement strategies; maps community assets and develops co-creation protocols.
Community Engagement Lab.quote: Shifted from consulting communities to partnering with them. Program relevance and uptake measurably improved.

Cost-Effectiveness Analysis Tool.desc: Comparative cost-effectiveness modeling platform; supports cost per outcome calculations and program prioritization decisions.
Cost-Effectiveness Analysis Tool.quote: Justified program budget allocation to leadership using comparative cost-effectiveness. Credible and defensible.

Cost Effectiveness 101.desc: Foundational concepts in cost-effectiveness analysis for policy decisions and program prioritization.
Cost Effectiveness 101.quote: Clear, focused, and immediately useful for policy analysis.

Media for Development: Communication, Po.desc: Covers ethical representation, humanitarian communication, participatory media models (PARI, Khabar Lahariya), data journalism, and AI/deepfake considerations for development practitioners.
Media for Development: Communication, Po.quote: Completely changed how our team approaches field documentation and beneficiary representation. Essential for anyone producing development content.

VaniScribe: AI Transcription for Develop.desc: Transcribe field recordings in 10+ South Asian languages using Sarvam AI. Speaker diarization, timestamping, and structured exports for qualitative analysis.
VaniScribe: AI Transcription for Develop.quote: Transcribed 40 hours of Hindi FGDs in a weekend. Diarization was surprisingly accurate for group discussions.
